title: Today's AI Brief: Visa invests in Replit to power agentic payments for developers
summary: A look at Visa's investment in Replit and the Vatican's AI encyclical presentation
imageAlt: Image from source article for Visa invests in Replit to power agentic payments for developers
imageCaption: Visa invests in Replit to power agentic payments for developers
imageSourceTitle: Visa invests in Replit to power agentic payments for developers

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm here to walk you through some fresh AI news. First up, Visa has made an undisclosed investment in Replit, an AI coding platform. What's interesting here is that they're not just investing - they're also exploring ways to integrate Visa's payment products into Replit. This means developers, and even the AI agents they build, could make payments directly within the platform.

According to TechCrunch, this move could enable 'agentic payments' - think of it like payments powered by AI agents. Imagine building an AI that can not only code but also handle transactions on its own. It's a platform shift that could make development workflows much smoother. For instance, developers could focus on building their applications without worrying about handling payments manually. It's a game-changer for the development community.

In other news, the Vatican has taken a step into the AI world. They invited Christopher Olah, co-founder of Anthropic, to speak at the presentation of the Pope's AI encyclical. This is a big deal - it's not every day you see the Vatican and a cutting-edge AI company like Anthropic teaming up. The Pope's encyclical on AI is a significant move, and having someone from Anthropic there adds some weight to the discussion. It's clear that AI is becoming a topic of interest beyond just the tech world.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
